LanguageData.MetalhorrorSuspicion.label: metalhorror suspicion
LanguageData.MetalhorrorSuspicion.instruction: write this as an unresolved colony horror: keep it grounded in what the pawn can observe, carry suspicion into ordinary details, and do not name hidden game mechanics beyond the supplied facts
LanguageData.AncientDanger.label: ancient danger
LanguageData.AncientDanger.instruction: write the pawn's immediate foreboding at the ancient wall as one personal moment; keep the danger uncertain and do not reveal what is inside
LanguageData.VoidMonolithDiscovery.label: void monolith discovery
LanguageData.VoidMonolithDiscovery.instruction: write the pawn's first discovery of the void monolith as extreme dark anomaly dread; make the structure feel inhuman and psychic, but keep the moment grounded in what the pawn can see and feel
LanguageData.VoidMonolithActivation.label: void monolith activation
LanguageData.VoidMonolithActivation.instruction: write the pawn's completed interaction with the void monolith as extreme dark anomaly dread; focus on the psychic pressure, the structure changing, and the sense that something beyond the colony has noticed
LanguageData.Birthday.label: birthday
LanguageData.Birthday.instruction: write the pawn's birthday as an uneasy RimWorld milestone, not a celebration; focus on age, fragility, and what another year can cost on the rim
LanguageData.HeartAttack.label: heart attack
LanguageData.HeartAttack.instruction: write the pawn's heart attack as immediate bodily danger; keep it personal, physical, and urgent without inventing treatment outcomes
LanguageData.PrisonBreak.label: prison break
LanguageData.PrisonBreak.instruction: write the colony's reaction to a prison break as sudden danger and alarm; focus on the pawn's position in the colony response, not the prisoners' inner thoughts
